name: Beijing Hutong Courtyard Inn
notes: A converted siheyuan in the heart of Nanluoguxiang hutong. Traditional grey-brick courtyard, heated kang beds, and a communal tea room. Five-minute walk to the Drum Tower.

name: Xi'an Grand Tang Hotel
notes: Modern hotel directly opposite the Tang Paradise park, within walking distance of the Big Wild Goose Pagoda and the Muslim Quarter night market.

name: Chengdu Panda Inn
notes: Boutique guesthouse in the Yulin district, Chengdu's most local neighbourhood. Great base for panda base early morning visits. Rooftop terrace with city views.

name: Chongqing Yangtze View Hotel
notes: Perched on the Yuzhong peninsula with panoramic views of the Yangtze–Jialing confluence. Rooftop terrace perfect for watching the neon city come alive after dark.

name: Zhangjiajie Forest Lodge
notes: Timber-and-stone lodge on the edge of the national park. Rooms with floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking the pillar peaks. Provides shuttle to park entrances from 6 am.

name: Guilin Li River Guesthouse
notes: Family-run guesthouse on the Li River bank in Yangshuo. Bicycles included, river views from every room, and the owner's mother cooks Guilin rice noodles for breakfast.

name: Hong Kong Central Hostel
notes: Serviced apartments in Sheung Wan, steps from Central MTR and the Mid-Levels escalator. Compact but well-equipped — the ideal base for exploring both sides of the harbour.
